trade.summary: Italy and Poland have strong and growing bilateral trade as Poland's economy has rapidly expanded. Italy exports machinery, motor vehicles, chemicals, and food; Poland exports machinery, furniture, food products, and transport equipment. Poland is one of Italy's most important Central/Eastern European trade partners.

diplomatic.summary: Both are EU and NATO members with generally aligned positions on European integration and Western security. Poland and Italy differ on migration policy — Poland rejects EU migration quotas that Italy supports for burden-sharing purposes. On Russia and Ukraine, Poland has been more hawkish than Italy. Despite these differences, the relationship is fundamentally positive within the EU framework.
recent_incidents: Disagreements over EU migration policy (2024 EU pact negotiations)

military.summary: Both are NATO allies. Poland's rapid defense buildup since 2022 and Italy's NATO commitments create coordination opportunities. Italy and Poland cooperate within NATO frameworks. Italy has supported eastern flank reinforcement measures that benefit Poland.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. Italian companies (in manufacturing, retail, banking) are among the largest EU investors in Poland. Unicredit, Intesa Sanpaolo, and other Italian banks have Polish subsidiaries. Polish investment in Italy is growing. Poland's structural fund receipts from EU partly flow through Italy's capital markets.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Both countries are focused on energy diversification away from Russia. ENI has limited Polish operations. Italy and Poland broadly align on EU energy security policy.

scientific.summary: Active scientific cooperation through Horizon Europe. Joint research in engineering, materials science, and environmental studies. Large Polish community in Italy has created cultural and academic linkages.
